name: Exa MCP Server
what_it_does: AI-native search engine optimized for agent use, providing web search, content retrieval, and semantic search capabilities via an official MCP server.
use_cases: Web search and content retrieval in agent workflows
Research and fact-finding for AI agents
Grounding LLM responses with current web content
Semantic search over curated content sources
Finding recent news and events for agents
best_when: An agent needs to search the web for current information, retrieve full page content, or find semantically similar documents.
avoid_when: You need to search private databases or internal documentation — Exa only indexes the public web.

auth.notes: Single API key via EXA_API_KEY environment variable. Simple header-based auth. No scope granularity — all operations share one key.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-search pricing; team and enterprise plans available
pricing.notes: Free tier sufficient for development and low-volume agents. Usage-based pricing scales well.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; developer tier rate-limited

known_agent_gotchas: No scope granularity — single API key grants full access; rotate if compromised
Rate limits vary by plan and are not always clearly documented in advance
Content retrieval (full page text) is a separate operation from search — costs extra credits
Search results include AI-curated sources; may miss very new content not yet indexed
Neural search mode (semantic) can behave differently from keyword mode — test both for your use case
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key model without scopes is a minor concern for multi-agent deployments — use separate keys per agent.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS only. No scope granularity on API keys — all requests use same key. Recommend using separate API keys per agent deployment.
